steps.title: Identify the participle and what it must modify
steps.content: The sentence begins with “First assigned to Britain in the 1960s,” an introductory participial phrase. A participle at the start of a sentence must modify the subject of the main clause that follows—here, the phrase describes the prefix being assigned, so the main clause's subject must be the prefix.

steps.title: Match the participle to the sentence's subject
steps.content: Only D has the prefix as the main clause's subject: “the prefix was used by only switchboard operators initially.” The other choices make “international direct dialing” (A and B) or “switchboard operators” (C) the subject, breaking the required participle-subject connection. That matches choice D—D is correct.
